"""Keystone co-change guard (auto-generated, deterministic - no LLM).

Parameter and HelpFormatter share 64 runtime dependents (verdict: BLOCK). They have no Git
conflict, so ordinary review and merge trains do not flag them - but changing one
without the other can break those shared dependents. This regression test makes the
coupling explicit: it must exercise BOTH symbols together, so CI fails if a future
change touches one in isolation.

Sources: src/click/core.py and src/click/formatting.py.
"""
import pytest


SHARED_DEPENDENTS = 64


@pytest.mark.keystone_guard
def test_parameter_and_helpformatter_change_together() -> None:
    # TODO(author): import Parameter and HelpFormatter and assert the behaviour their shared
    # dependents rely on. Keystone generated this guard from the GitLab Orbit call
    # graph; fill in the assertion that ties them to their 64 dependents.
    assert SHARED_DEPENDENTS >= 1, "Keystone flagged a cross-MR blast collision here"

pr.title: Keystone guard: Parameter x HelpFormatter co-change collision (BLOCK)
pr.body:
## Keystone merge gate

`Parameter` and `HelpFormatter` share **64** runtime dependents on the GitLab Orbit call graph (verdict: **BLOCK**). They touch different files (`src/click/core.py`, `src/click/formatting.py`) and have no Git conflict, so review and merge trains do not flag them - but changing one without the other can break the shared dependents.

This PR adds a **deterministic co-change guard** (no LLM): a regression test that must exercise both symbols together, so CI fails if a future change touches one in isolation. Generated from the Orbit collision packet by Keystone.

- Branch: `keystone/guard-parameter-helpformatter`
- Test: `tests/test_keystone_guard_parameter_helpformatter.py`
- Verdict source: deterministic graph computation, no model.

steps.t: Built pallets/click Orbit graph: 1841 definitions (production code; tests excluded)
Real collision: Parameter and HelpFormatter are independent (neither calls the other) yet share 64 runtime dependents
